# Acme Corporation — Q3 2024 Financial Summary

## Executive Summary

Acme Corporation reported strong Q3 2024 results, with total revenue of $128.5 million, up 12% year-over-year. Net income reached $18.2 million, with an operating margin of 14.2%. The company's cloud infrastructure segment drove the majority of growth.

## Revenue Breakdown

### By Segment
- Cloud Services: $72.3 million (56.3% of total revenue)
- Enterprise Software: $38.1 million (29.6% of total revenue)
- Professional Services: $18.1 million (14.1% of total revenue)

### By Region
- North America: $76.8 million (59.8%)
- Europe: $33.4 million (26.0%)
- Asia-Pacific: $18.3 million (14.2%)

## Capital Expenditures

| Quarter | Amount | Primary Driver |
|---------|--------|----------------|
| Q1 | $32,000,000 | R&D |
| Q2 | $41,000,000 | Acquisitions |
| Q3 | $45,000,000 | Cloud Infrastructure |

Total year-to-date CapEx: $118,000,000

## Key Financial Metrics

- Gross Margin: 68.5%
- Operating Margin: 14.2%
- Free Cash Flow: $22.7 million
- Cash and Equivalents: $156.3 million
- Total Debt: $45.0 million
- Debt-to-Equity Ratio: 0.32
- Earnings Per Share (EPS): $1.82
- Total Shares Outstanding: 10,000,000

## Employee Headcount

Total employees: 2,847
- Engineering: 1,240
- Sales & Marketing: 685
- Operations: 512
- G&A: 410

## Travel and Expense Policy

### Air Travel
- Executive level: Business class permitted up to $5,000 per flight
- Senior Manager level: Premium economy permitted up to $2,500 per flight
- Standard employees: Economy class only, maximum $1,200 per flight
- All international flights over 8 hours: Business class upgrade permitted for Senior Manager and above

### Hotel Policy
- Maximum nightly rate: $250 for standard employees, $400 for executives
- Extended stays (5+ nights): 15% discount negotiated rate required

### Meal Allowances
- Domestic travel: $75 per day
- International travel: $100 per day
- Client entertainment: Up to $150 per person, requires manager approval

## Q4 Outlook

The company projects Q4 revenue between $135-140 million, representing 15-18% year-over-year growth. Cloud Services segment is expected to grow 25% in Q4. The company plans to hire 200 additional engineers in Q4.
